# Questions and answers on benzene impurities

## 1. What are the limits?
The acceptable intake (AI) for benzene in finished products is 2 ng/day,
which corresponds to a virtually safe dose level derived from the
TD50 of 67 mg/kg/day in mice. Applicants should justify their control
strategy in line with ICH M7.

## 2. When are tests required?
Benzene testing is required when:
- the API synthesis uses class 1 solvents,
- excipients are known to introduce trace benzene,
- the manufacturing process involves polymerisation initiators that
  may release benzene as a degradation product.

A risk-based approach is acceptable provided the rationale is
documented in the dossier.

## 3. How should controls be set?
Applicants are expected to set specifications consistent with the
control approach described in ICH Q3D. A periodic verification strategy
may be acceptable when the impurity is unlikely to exceed 30 % of the
permitted daily exposure.
